skill.name: hook-development
skill.description: Guides developers through creating, testing, and validating Claude Code plugin hooks for event-driven automation and policy enforcement.
skill.summary: Build, test, and validate Claude Code plugin hooks for safer development workflows.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: This official Anthropic skill provides instructional material and helper scripts for Claude Code hook development. Static indicators reflect expected hook capabilities, including shell execution, file checks, environment variables, and optional integrations. No prompt injection attempt or malicious behavior was confirmed.

content.value_statement: Developers need reliable hooks to enforce workflow rules without breaking normal work. This skill explains hook formats, events, testing, and validation for Claude Code plugins.
actual_capabilities: Explains plugin and settings hook configuration formats.
Covers PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, SubagentStop, SessionStart, and related events.
Provides prompt-based hook patterns for context-aware validation.
Provides command hook examples for deterministic checks and integrations.
Includes scripts to lint hooks, test hook input, and validate hook schemas.
Shows patterns for blocking risky commands and sensitive file writes.
limitations: The skill teaches hook development but does not install hooks automatically.
Command hook examples require shell, jq, and local execution permissions.
Prompt-based hook quality depends on the prompt and available event context.
External service examples require users to configure credentials and endpoints.

use_cases.title: Add safety checks to plugin tools
use_cases.description: Create PreToolUse hooks that review Bash, Write, and Edit actions before Claude Code runs them.

use_cases.title: Validate task completion before stopping
use_cases.description: Use Stop hooks to check that tests, builds, or acceptance criteria are complete before work ends.

use_cases.title: Prepare project context at session start
use_cases.description: Load project type, build system, and environment context when a Claude Code session begins.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic hook
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new plugin hook.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple Claude Code PreToolUse hook for my plugin. Explain the plugin hooks.json wrapper and the hook event structure.

prompt_templates.title: Validate file writes
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate safety check for Write and Edit tools.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a hook that reviews Write and Edit operations. It should block path traversal and ask before sensitive files are changed.

prompt_templates.title: Test an existing hook
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced debugging of hook scripts and outputs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my hook script and test input. Find schema issues, exit code problems, timeout risks, and output format errors.

prompt_templates.title: Build a hook policy system
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced multi-event workflow with prompt and command hooks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a Claude Code plugin hook system using PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, and Stop events for a regulated repository.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for a hook that blocks destructive Bash commands.
output_examples.output: The response outlines a PreToolUse Bash matcher, denial criteria for destructive commands, test cases, and deployment steps.

output_examples.input: A team needs a Stop hook that checks quality gates.
output_examples.output: The response describes a prompt-based Stop hook that verifies tests, build status, unresolved questions, and completion evidence.

output_examples.input: A plugin author wants to validate a hooks configuration file.
output_examples.output: The response identifies wrapper format requirements, supported event names, timeout guidance, and validation script usage.

best_practices: Use prompt hooks for context-aware policy decisions and command hooks for fast deterministic checks.
Test hooks with representative inputs before enabling them in a plugin.
Keep hook outputs valid, concise, and aligned with the event decision format.
anti_patterns: Do not run long command hooks without a strict timeout.
Do not hardcode absolute paths when plugin-relative paths are available.
Do not trust raw hook input without validating expected fields.

faq.question: What are Claude Code hooks?
faq.answer: Hooks are event-driven checks or actions that run around Claude Code workflow events.

faq.question: When should I use prompt-based hooks?
faq.answer: Use them when a decision needs context, natural language reasoning, or flexible policy review.

faq.question: When should I use command hooks?
faq.answer: Use them for fast checks, local scripts, filesystem validation, and external tool integrations.

faq.question: Does this skill create production-ready hooks automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides implementation and provides examples that users must adapt and test.

faq.question: Can hooks block unsafe tool use?
faq.answer: Yes. PreToolUse hooks can deny, allow, or ask before selected tool calls continue.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. It is an official instructional skill, and the executable examples support expected hook development tasks.
